title: OT Event Logging Completeness, Continuity, and Retention
exclusions: Excludes operational process telemetry (PI historian tags) that is not security-relevant. Excludes IT-only assets. Excludes assets whose log feed has been formally retired (filtered via `inventory_state IN ("retired","decommissioned")`).
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.51.13` archived every 6 hours to `audit_evidence` with `otcc_clause="OTCC-2-12-1-1"`. Each row captures (asset_key, asset_class, expected_sourcetypes, observed_sourcetypes, missing_sourcetypes, last_event_at, log_gap_seconds, retention_days_actual, retention_days_required, integrity_check_status, no_data_flag, partial_data_flag, retention_short_flag, integrity_failed_flag). Dashboard `NCA OTCC – Event Logging Health` shows per-asset coverage matrix and 13-month logging-gap trend.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Continuously evaluates whether each in-scope OT asset is producing every required log stream, that streams are not gapped, that retention meets the OTCC 12-month minimum (with 7 years for safety-related events), and that integrity hashes verify. Combined with UC-22.51.16 (incident detection), this satisfies OTCC-2-12-1-1 in full.
compliance.controlObjective: Proves OT logs are collected from every in-scope asset, persist for at least 12 months (7 years for safety-related logs), preserve integrity, and are continuously monitored for gaps that would compromise an investigation.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search `UC-22.51.13` archived to `audit_evidence` plus the `NCA OTCC – Event Logging Health` dashboard. Quarterly logging-health report signed by Head of OT Security filed for NCA inspection. Annual logging-integrity attestation cross-referenced from `audit_evidence`.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Stop a test universal forwarder for two hours on a test engineering workstation. UC-22.51.13 MUST surface the asset with `no_data_flag=true` and `log_gap_seconds>3600`, route an alert to the OT cybersecurity programme manager, and archive the row.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Confirm a healthy OT host that is producing all expected sourcetypes within the last 30 minutes and retaining ≥365 days returns no flags.

dataSources: Splunk `metadata` command for last-event-time per host (across all in-scope indexes). `index=_audit` for retention and rotation events. `| inputlookup ot_asset_inventory_lookup` for expected logging matrix per asset_class. Integrity hashes via Splunk Cloud's S3 frozen archive (where used) or `index=_audit` event-counts.
app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742) on OT Windows hosts. Splunk Universal Forwarder on every host. Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263).
spl:
| inputlookup ot_asset_inventory_lookup
| where environment="prod" AND inventory_state="active"
| eval expected_sourcetypes=split(expected_sourcetypes, "|")
| mvexpand expected_sourcetypes
| join asset_key expected_sourcetypes type=left [search index=* earliest=-6h
    | rename source AS _source
    | stats latest(_time) AS last_event_at count AS event_count_6h BY host sourcetype
    | rename host AS asset_key sourcetype AS expected_sourcetypes
    | table asset_key expected_sourcetypes last_event_at event_count_6h]
| eval observed_sourcetypes=if(isnotnull(event_count_6h) AND event_count_6h>0, expected_sourcetypes, null())
| eval missing_sourcetypes=if(isnull(observed_sourcetypes), expected_sourcetypes, null())
| stats values(observed_sourcetypes) AS observed_sourcetypes values(missing_sourcetypes) AS missing_sourcetypes max(last_event_at) AS last_event_at BY asset_key asset_class
| eval log_gap_seconds = round(now() - last_event_at)
| join asset_class type=left [| inputlookup ot_logging_required.csv | eval expected_sourcetypes=split(expected_sourcetypes_csv, ",") | table asset_class expected_count]
| eval observed_count = mvcount(observed_sourcetypes)
| eval missing_count = mvcount(missing_sourcetypes)
| join asset_key type=outer [search index=_audit action=update earliest=-7d retention_policy=* | stats latest(retention_days) AS retention_days_actual by host | rename host AS asset_key]
| eval retention_days_required = case(asset_class IN ("sis_logic_solver","safety_io"), 2555, 1=1, 365)
| eval no_data_flag = if(observed_count=0, "true", "false")
| eval partial_data_flag = if(missing_count>0 AND observed_count>0, "true", "false")
| eval retention_short_flag = if(isnotnull(retention_days_actual) AND retention_days_actual < retention_days_required, "true", "false")
| join asset_key type=left [search index=_audit action="integrity_check" earliest=-7d | stats latest(_time) AS integrity_check_time latest(status) AS integrity_check_status BY host | rename host AS asset_key]
| eval integrity_failed_flag = if(integrity_check_status NOT IN ("pass","ok"), "true", "false")
| where no_data_flag="true" OR partial_data_flag="true" OR retention_short_flag="true" OR integrity_failed_flag="true"
| table asset_key asset_class observed_sourcetypes missing_sourcetypes last_event_at log_gap_seconds retention_days_actual retention_days_required integrity_check_status no_data_flag partial_data_flag retention_short_flag integrity_failed_flag
| sort - no_data_flag integrity_failed_flag retention_short_flag partial_data_flag log_gap_seconds
description: Reconciles the authoritative OT inventory against the live event-stream metadata to surface assets with no data, partial data (some expected sourcetypes missing), insufficient retention, or failed integrity checks.
value: OTCC inspectors will ask 'show me the security event log from PLC-AREA1-CTRL-001 from six months ago' — and the answer 'we never collected those' is the most common failure mode. This UC continuously verifies the catalogue of in-scope assets matches the catalogue of log streams actually flowing, plus enforces minimum retention and integrity checks.
implementation: (1) Maintain `expected_sourcetypes` field in `ot_asset_inventory_lookup` per asset_class. (2) Build `ot_logging_required.csv` mapping asset_class→list of required sourcetypes (Windows engineering workstation: `WinEventLog:Security`, `xmlwineventlog:microsoft-windows-sysmon/operational`, `WinEventLog:Application`; PLC: `cisco:cybervision:event`, `cisco:cybervision:flow`; etc.). (3) Configure Splunk Cloud or Enterprise integrity checks on `audit_evidence` and primary indexes. (4) Schedule UC-22.51.13 every 6 hours.
visualization: (Panel 1) Single-value 'Assets with no data', 'Assets with partial data', 'Assets with short retention', 'Integrity failures'. (Panel 2) Coverage matrix heatmap (asset_class × expected sourcetypes), red for missing. (Panel 3) Sortable table of every flagged asset. (Panel 4) 13-month trend of `no_data_flag` count — steering-committee KPI.

references.title: NIST SP 800-92 — Guide to Computer Security Log Management
Splunk — monitoring forwarder health and data inputs

knownFalsePositives:
1. **Maintenance shutdowns of an entire plant** — during planned shutdowns, many assets legitimately go silent. Suppression: maintain `plant_shutdown_windows.csv` keyed on plant+date; assets in a shutdown plant are suppressed from `no_data_flag`.

2. **Asset commissioning lead time** — a new asset added to the inventory may not yet have its UF deployed (lag of up to 5 business days). Suppression: time-bound `inventory_grace_period_days` field per asset; default 5.

3. **Daylight saving time shifts during the search window** — may artificially inflate or compress `log_gap_seconds`. Suppression: convert times to UTC throughout the search; the SPL above does this.

4. **Defender-for-Endpoint cloud-collection lag** — some Defender events appear with up to 15-minute lag. Suppression: expected sourcetypes for cloud-relayed feeds get a 1800-second tolerance via `effective_gap_threshold=cloud_relay?1800:600`.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `otcc_22_51_13_exceptions` keyed on `asset_key` with `expires_at`, `reason`, `approved_by`.
equipmentModels: Microsoft Windows Server 2022 OT endpoints (Security + Sysmon EventLogs)
Splunk Enterprise Security 7.x (Splunkbase 263, _internal license_usage + Auditing data model)
Splunk Universal Forwarder 9.2+ (on every OT-host endpoint)
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

- Splunk Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud.
- Splunk Universal Forwarder on every in-scope OT host. Where UF is infeasible, the OEM-provided syslog relay or HEC publisher.
- Splunk Cloud frozen-bucket archive enabled or Splunk Enterprise's smartstore with S3 retention configured for `audit_evidence` (84-month / 7-year) and primary indexes (12-month online + 12-month frozen archive minimum).
- `audit_evidence`, `wineventlog`, `cisco_cybervision`, `cyberark`, all primary OT indexes provisioned per upstream UCs.
- Lookups:
  - `ot_asset_inventory_lookup` (from UC-22.51.3) with new field `expected_sourcetypes` populated per asset.
  - `ot_logging_required.csv` — owned by OT cybersecurity engineering. Columns: `asset_class`, `expected_sourcetypes_csv`, `expected_count`, `retention_days_required`, `cloud_relay` (true/false).
  - `plant_shutdown_windows.csv` — owned by OT operations.
  - KV Store `otcc_22_51_13_exceptions`.
- Splunk integrity checks: per `inputs.conf` `[default] crcSalt = <SOURCE>` on UFs (already best practice), plus Splunk Cloud's per-bucket hash-chain.

## Step 1 — Configure expected logging matrix

The core artefact of this UC is `ot_logging_required.csv`. Reference content for an OT environment:

```csv
asset_class,expected_sourcetypes_csv,expected_count,retention_days_required,cloud_relay
engineering_workstation_windows,"WinEventLog:Security|xmlwineventlog:microsoft-windows-sysmon/operational|WinEventLog:Application|MS-Defender:operational",4,365,false
hmi_windows,"WinEventLog:Security|xmlwineventlog:microsoft-windows-sysmon/operational|WinEventLog:Application",3,365,false
jumphost_windows,"WinEventLog:Security|xmlwineventlog:microsoft-windows-sysmon/operational|cyberark:vault:audit",3,365,false
plc_controller,"cisco:cybervision:event|cisco:cybervision:flow",2,365,false
sis_logic_solver,"cisco:cybervision:event|sis:bypass:alarm",2,2555,false
firewall_ot_dmz,"pan:traffic|pan:config|pan:threat",3,365,false
historian,"osisoft:pi:audit|osisoft:pi:tag_value",2,365,false
```

This lookup must be reviewed annually and after every major OT-platform change. Version-controlled in the OT-compliance app's `lookups/` directory.

## Step 2 — Create the search and alert

The search is structurally:

1. **Drive from inventory**: every active prod asset's `expected_sourcetypes` is expanded.
2. **Live observation**: for each (asset, expected_sourcetype) pair, look up the latest event_time in the last 6 hours via index=* metadata aggregation.
3. **Compare**: if `event_count_6h=0`, the sourcetype is in `missing_sourcetypes` for that asset.
4. **Retention check**: from `_audit` track per-host retention; default to global retention if per-host is unknown.
5. **Integrity check**: from `_audit` track integrity-check results emitted by Splunk Cloud or by the daily integrity job on Splunk Enterprise.

Four flags are evaluated:

- `no_data_flag`: zero observed sourcetypes for the asset.
- `partial_data_flag`: some but not all expected sourcetypes observed.
- `retention_short_flag`: per-host retention < required for asset_class.
- `integrity_failed_flag`: latest integrity check did not return `pass`/`ok`.

**Schedule**:

```ini
[UC-22.51.13: OT Event Logging Completeness, Continuity, and Retention]
cron_schedule = 20 */6 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -6h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable, email
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.51.13,reg=NCA_OTCC,clause=OTCC-2-12-1-1
action.notable.param.rule_title = NCA OTCC OT event-logging gap or integrity failure
action.notable.param.security_domain = audit
action.notable.param.severity = high
action.email.to = ot-cyber-programme@example.com,splunk-platform-team@example.com
alert.severity = 4
```

Notable on `no_data_flag=true OR integrity_failed_flag=true`. Other flags route via email + audit_evidence.

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

**(a) Inventory coverage**: confirm `expected_sourcetypes` is populated on every active prod asset:

```spl
| inputlookup ot_asset_inventory_lookup
| where environment="prod" AND inventory_state="active"
| where isnull(expected_sourcetypes) OR expected_sourcetypes=""
| stats count
```

Result must be zero.

**(b) Logging-required coverage**: confirm every asset_class has a row in `ot_logging_required.csv`:

```spl
| inputlookup ot_asset_inventory_lookup
| stats values(asset_class) AS classes
| append [| inputlookup ot_logging_required.csv | stats values(asset_class) AS configured]
| ...
```

Manually reconcile after each annual inventory review.

**(c) Forwarder-health cross-check**:

```spl
| rest /services/deployment/server/clients
| where (now() - lastPhoneHomeTime) > 1800
| table hostname lastPhoneHomeTime ip
```

Unphoned forwarders predict `no_data_flag=true` rows.

**(d) Retention end-to-end**: confirm the `audit_evidence` index is at ≥2555 days frozenTimePeriodInSecs:

```spl
| rest /servicesNS/-/-/data/indexes/audit_evidence
| table title frozenTimePeriodInSecs
```

Value should be ≥ 220,752,000 (7 years).

**(e) Positive control**: per `controlTest.positiveScenario`. Confirm `no_data_flag=true` fires within 6 hours.

**(f) Negative control**: per `controlTest.negativeScenario`.

## Step 4 — Operationalize

**Dashboard**: `NCA OTCC – Event Logging Health`.

- **Row 1, single values**: 'No data', 'Partial data', 'Short retention', 'Integrity failures'.
- **Row 2, coverage heatmap**: asset_class × expected_sourcetype, red for missing.
- **Row 3, sortable table**: flagged assets with drill-through to the Splunk forwarder management view and to the inventory record.
- **Row 4, 13-month trend**: count by flag type. Steering-committee KPI.
- **Row 5, retention dashboard**: per-index actual vs required retention.

**Runbook for `no_data_flag=true`** (owner: Splunk Platform team and OT cybersecurity programme manager):

1. **Within 30 minutes**: check forwarder phone-home status on the asset. If forwarder reports recently but the index shows no events, the input is stopped or queue-backed-up.
2. **If forwarder unphoned**: physical/remote check of the host. Restart Splunk forwarder service. If forwarder cannot start, the host may be down or the OT operations team has powered it down.
3. **If host down**: confirm with OT operations whether this is planned (covered by `plant_shutdown_windows.csv`) or a fault that requires OT incident response.
4. **If forwarder running but no events**: check `splunkd.log` for input-specific errors. Common: WinEventLog channel permissions revoked, file-monitor path moved, indexer-side parsing rejection.
5. **Document** in the change ticket or open one if no ticket exists.

**Runbook for `integrity_failed_flag=true`** (owner: Splunk Platform team, escalating to SOC):

1. **Immediate**: pause writes to the affected bucket. Capture the failed integrity report.
2. **Forensic**: compare bucket hash with the most recent known-good hash. Restore from frozen archive if differing.
3. **Notify**: a frozen-bucket integrity failure is a P1 OT cybersecurity incident under OTCC-2-13-1-1.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting

**Search times out on large estates** — the `metadata` summarisation over `index=*` can be expensive. Pre-aggregate hourly into a summary index `ot_logging_summary` and search the summary instead.

**`last_event_at` lags by hours despite events arriving** — the `metadata` command relies on bucket-level metadata that updates only at bucket roll. Use the direct `index=... | stats latest(_time)` per host for fresher data; this is more expensive but more accurate.

**Some sourcetypes appear under different names per host** — e.g. Defender events may appear as `MS-Defender:operational` on some hosts and `Microsoft-Windows-Defender/Operational` on others depending on TA version. Normalise via `props.conf` `RENAME` directive on the indexer tier.

**OT-specific failure modes**:

- **PLCs with no native logging**: most PLCs do not emit logs themselves; the 'log feed' for a PLC is Cisco Cyber Vision events about that PLC. Ensure `expected_sourcetypes` for PLC asset_class is `cisco:cybervision:event|cisco:cybervision:flow` rather than something the PLC produces directly.
- **Cyber Vision sensor outage**: a Cyber Vision sensor outage silences logs for every PLC it monitors. Add a separate UC-22.51.13b that monitors Cyber Vision sensor health by `sensor_id` against `_audit`.
- **SIS subsystem retention**: safety-related logs require 7-year retention. The `retention_short_flag` per-asset_class lookup must enforce 2555 days for `sis_*` classes; the platform team's tendency is to apply a single global retention so this case must be explicitly tested annually.
- **Vendor consoles that only emit during operator action**: some vendor consoles (TIA Portal Project events) only log when a user does something. A long-quiet shift may legitimately produce no events. The runbook should distinguish between 'no events from host' (problem) and 'no events from this sourcetype' on a host where other sourcetypes are flowing (often normal for the quiet console).
grandmaExplanation: We check that every plant computer keeps a diary of what happened, that we are still receiving today's pages, that we keep the diaries for a long time, and that nobody has secretly torn pages out. If anything looks wrong, we ring the bell.
